evals.name: greenfield-drone-propulsion
evals.prompt: I'm starting a small electric drone project. Can you model the propulsion subsystem in SysML v2? It has a brushless motor, a LiPo battery, and a propeller. Capture each as a part with sensible engineering attributes — at least mass for all three, plus power draw for the motor and stored energy for the battery. Save it to drone_propulsion.sysml.
evals.expected_output: A valid .sysml file with a package containing part defs for Motor, Battery, Propeller (and likely a PropulsionSubsystem composing them), with mass/power/energy attributes typed by real ISQ quantity value types. Compiles with zero errors.

assertions.text: The produced .sysml file compiles with zero ERROR diagnostics (validate_sysml_file ok=true)
Quantity/unit references use real standard-library qualified names (e.g. ISQ/ISQBase/SI/ScalarValues), with no invented names like ISQ::weight or SI::kgs
Contains at least three part defs covering motor, battery, and propeller
Mass is represented for all three parts; motor has a power attribute and battery has an energy attribute

evals.name: requirements-battery-spec
evals.prompt: Turn this battery spec into a SysML v2 requirements model: the battery's usable energy shall be at least 500 Wh, and its mass shall not exceed 2 kg. I want proper requirement definitions with a Battery subject and the limits expressed as constraints with the correct units, so we can later check a design against them.
evals.expected_output: A valid .sysml file with requirement defs that declare a subject (a Battery part), express the energy and mass limits as require/assume constraints using SI units, and reference real ISQ quantity types. Compiles clean.

assertions.text: The produced .sysml file compiles with zero ERROR diagnostics
Uses requirement def with a declared subject (the compiler does not flag 'Subject must be first parameter')
Energy and mass limits are expressed as constraints with unit literals using real SI units (e.g. value[Wh-equivalent] and value[kg]/SI::kg)
References real ISQ/SI qualified names, not invented ones

evals.name: audit-and-fix-broken-model
evals.prompt: Something's off with our vehicle model and it won't load in our tool. Can you check sysml/evals/fixtures/broken_vehicle.sysml, tell me exactly what's wrong, and give me a corrected version that's valid?
evals.expected_output: Correctly identifies the two defects — the 'part deff' keyword typo (syntax) and the invented 'ISQ::weight' reference (should be ISQ::mass) — explains them with locations, and produces a corrected model that compiles clean.

assertions.text: Identifies the 'part deff' typo as a syntax error and corrects it to 'part def'
Identifies that ISQ::weight does not resolve and corrects it to a real quantity (ISQ::mass / ISQBase::mass)
The corrected model compiles with zero ERROR diagnostics
Explanation references concrete locations/messages rather than vague guesses
